notices.html: Holland Ridge Sunflower Festival (Sept 10 to Oct 12) tickets go on sale Wednesday, Sept 9 at 10am, online only at hollandridgefarms.com. Nothing to pick there this weekend.

blurbs.terhune: This weekend: last weekend's first-ever Flower Festival has wrapped. Blueberries and pick-your-own peaches may be starting in the 2-acre patch, but call 609-924-2310 to confirm before you go, the spring freeze wiped out cherries. Store and donuts open 9 to 6, no pets.
blurbs.vonthun: This weekend: the Blueberry Festival wrapped July 13 to 14, and u-pick blueberries typically finish here by mid-July, so call 732-329-8656 to check if any fields are still open before driving over. Farm market and donuts open daily.
blurbs.hickory: This weekend: no specific 2026 crop update found online for this small, diversified farm. Mid to late July usually brings peaches, corn, tomatoes and other summer vegetables. Call 609-448-0629 for the day's pick. Open 9 to 6, Sundays 2 to 6, arrive by 5:15pm to enter the fields.
blurbs.hollandridge: Nothing to pick this weekend. The fall Sunflower Festival runs September 10 to October 12; tickets go on sale Wednesday, September 9 at 10am online only.
blurbs.locusthall: This weekend: blackberries ($8/qt) and peaches are self-serve picking Thursday to Sunday, 9am to 5pm, no entrance fee. A hayride carries you out to the fields; pay by QR code or cash on your way out.
blurbs.johnsonscorner: This weekend: blueberries are still picking (season runs mid-June through July), plus blackberries, new this year. Hayrides, animals and soft-serve on site. Picking dates can shift by a week or more, so call the Pick Your Own Hotline 609-654-5894 to confirm.
blurbs.battleview: This weekend: the spring freeze wiped out cherries, peaches, nectarines and apples this year, so likely no stone-fruit picking. The store may have corn and tomatoes; call 732-462-0756 first to confirm what's on hand.
blurbs.norzhill: This weekend: no confirmed u-pick crop yet. Their Sunflower Path opens Thursday, July 30 ($10pp, kids under 36in free, stems $2), not open yet this weekend. Call 908-371-2697 to confirm before heading over.
blurbs.alstede: This weekend: blueberries are in peak season and pick-your-own, along with other berries. Field admission runs $5.99 to $6.99pp depending on the day (free Mon to Thu before 11am). A 60-minute drive, so check their daily picking list or call before heading out.
blurbs.howell: This Saturday and Sunday: the farm is taken over by the free Mercer County 4-H Fair and Wheat Threshing, 10am to 8pm Saturday and 10am to 4pm Sunday. Animal shows, hayrides, pony rides, homemade ice cream and magic shows, free admission and parking, a canned good donation is welcomed.

events.title: Mercer County 4-H Fair & Wheat Threshing
events.desc: A full farm fair with animal shows, hayrides, pony rides, homemade ice cream and magic shows

events.title: Warren County Farmers' Fair & Hot Air Balloon Festival
events.time: gates 11am, fair noon to 10pm, balloon launch ~6:30pm
events.desc: A week-long county fair with a mass hot air balloon launch nearly every evening, weather permitting

events.title: Butterfly Day at DeKorte Park
events.desc: Guided butterfly walks, a costume contest, games and crafts in the Meadowlands

events.title: Mercer County Summer Concert Series
events.place: Mercer County Park Festival Grounds, West Windsor
events.desc: Lawn concert at the Festival Grounds, bring chairs
